title: Employees Are Adopting AI Faster Than Company Governance
intent: Capture the operational gap between employee AI usage and enterprise rollout discipline.

source.title: Microsoft and LinkedIn release the 2024 Work Trend Index on the state of AI at work
source.methodology: Survey of 31,000 people across 31 countries, LinkedIn labor data, Microsoft 365 productivity signals, and Fortune 500 customer research.

fact.primary_stat: 75% of knowledge workers said they already use AI at work.
supporting_stats: 79% of leaders said AI adoption is critical to remain competitive.
60% said their company lacks a vision and plan to implement AI.
78% of AI users said they bring their own AI tools to work.

business_relevance: AI adoption can emerge bottom-up faster than governance, architecture, or training.
Shadow AI creates productivity gains and data risk at the same time.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is directly relevant to AMS and support organizations. If people are already using AI but the company still lacks a plan, the result is fragmented prompts, weak auditability, and knowledge leakage. The enterprise task is not to suppress usage, but to channel it into approved workflows, retrieval, and guardrails.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Capture the operational gap between employee AI usage and enterprise rollout discipline.

attribution.preferred_citation: Dzmitryi Kharlanau. “Employees Are Adopting AI Faster Than Company Governance” (dataset bytes). CC BY-NC 4.0. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18862098. https://dkharlanau.github.io/datasets/ai-business-signals/aibs-002.json
